lyrics:
War and peace
Profit and poverty
Open and closed
Slave and free
Covert and overt
Fear and trust
Loud and still
Ash and dust

They don’t collide, they intertwine-
Into the outer-
Opposites attract in time

Love and hate
Push and pull
Tender and sharp
Empty and full
Sun and shadow
Rise and fall
Silk and iron
Nothing and all

They twist, they tangle, never match-
Into the outer-
Opposites attract in time

Truth and lies
High and low
Fast and slow
Yes and no
Order and chaos
Knife and thread
Breath and silence
Living and dead

Not black and white, but every crack-
Into the outer-
Opposites attract in time

Sacred and broken
Flesh and flame
Known and nameless
Pride and shame
Stone and water
Grace and lack
What pulls apart
Still circles back

The tether holds, the lines collapse-
Into the outer-
Opposites attract in time

Into the outer
Opposites attract in time
persona:
## PCBender

Rugged, wise, and deeply entrenched in the art of music-making, PCBender is more than just a musician—he’s a sonic craftsman. A guitar-slinging songwriter, producer, and storyteller, he embodies the soul of analog warmth, weaving together rich textures of classic rock, blues, and experimental soundscapes with a modern edge.

With a presence that carries the mystique of a seasoned explorer, PCBender is the Indiana Jones of music production—digging deep into the essence of sound, unearthing raw emotion, and shaping it into songs that resonate beyond time. His music is a fusion of grit and refinement, where worn-in guitar tones meet polished production, and every note feels like it was carved from experience.

A producer at heart, he doesn’t just create songs—he builds them. Each track is an expedition through layers of melody, distortion, and lyrical depth, reflecting both his technical expertise and his philosopher’s approach to songwriting. Whether delivering anthemic riffs, cinematic storytelling, or stripped-down acoustic wisdom, PCBender brings an authenticity that makes listeners feel like they’re sitting in the studio with him, watching music come to life.

As a key figure in Maricopa Records, PCBender bridges the gap between analog and AI-driven creativity, proving that the heart of a song is never about how it’s made, but why.

For those who seek music with depth, texture, and soul—PCBender is your guide.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/0aivrU155laeOcIoT4AhPo?si=pYdQp1J_S4y0vSFA4itgUg)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/pcbender/1793178641)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCvNXkiIHQJM-uY4MT3gmKDw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/PCBender.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track has a rich, layered texture rooted in vintage funk and soul, creating an atmosphere that is both confident and uplifting. The energy is consistently high and driving, propelled by a tight rhythm section that establishes a deep, danceable pocket groove. The production is clean and spacious, allowing each instrument clarity within a dense, powerful ensemble sound that has a classic warmth. The track is defined by its impressive instrumental breaks, particularly an explosive organ solo that shifts the focus before the song culminates in an extended, fiery guitar jam for the outro.

review.review_text: In 3/4 time at a steady 112 BPM, 'Opposites Attract in Time' finds PCBender doing what he does best: building a song from the inside out, letting the groove do the heavy lifting while the lyrics stack paradox on paradox until the hook lands with real weight. The G minor setting gives the verses a grounded tension that the chorus then releases cleanly, and the production stays spacious enough that you feel the rhythm section as much as hear it. 'Not black and white, but every crack' is the line that earns its keep, sharpening what could easily tip into cliche into something more honest about how contradiction actually works. A confident, well-constructed highlight.
anchors_used: lyric: 'Not black and white, but every crack'
